title: Field declarations
analysis:
This task adds field declarations to Reify — a new top-level declaration type representing typed mathematical fields (domain -> codomain mappings) with multiple source kinds (analytical, sampled, composed, imported). The implementation spans all layers: grammar, parser, types, compiler, evaluator, and stdlib.

Existing patterns to follow:
- Grammar: top-level declarations are listed in `_declaration` choice, member constructs follow seq() patterns with optional pub, keyword, name, etc.
- Parser (ts_parser.rs): CST->AST lowering via `Lowering` struct with `lower_*` methods, dispatched from `lower_source_file`.
- Syntax AST (lib.rs): Enums for declarations, structs for each decl type with name, is_pub, span, content_hash.
- Types (ty.rs): Type enum with variants like List(Box<Type>), Function{params, return_type}.
- Compiler (lib.rs): compile() function with pre-passes for enums/functions/traits, then main loop over declarations. compile_entity() handles structure/occurrence members.
- Eval (lib.rs): Engine::eval() processes templates with multi-pass (params -> lets -> guards).
- Stdlib (lib.rs): eval_builtin() dispatches by function name.

Field declarations are top-level (like structures, functions, traits), not member-level. The task specifies `field def name : DomainType -> CodomainType { source = kind { ... } }`. Fields evaluate to Value::Field values that wrap lambdas or sample data. Composition (Field<A,B> compose Field<B,C>) requires type checking that the codomain of the first matches the domain of the second.

Key design decisions:
1. Fields are top-level declarations (not members), added to `_declaration` in grammar and `Declaration` enum.
2. Type::Field { domain, codomain } added to the type system for type checking composition.
3. Value::Field variant added for runtime field values.
4. Field operations (gradient, divergence, curl, sample) added to stdlib.
5. Compiler produces CompiledField entries in CompiledModule (new vec alongside functions, traits, etc.).
6. Eval evaluates field declarations into Value::Field, storing them accessible by name.

prerequisites.description: Create test file skeletons: crates/reify-syntax/tests/field_tests.rs, crates/reify-compiler/tests/field_compile_tests.rs, crates/reify-eval/tests/field_eval_tests.rs with module imports and helper functions following existing test patterns

steps.description: Write failing parse test: parse_analytical_field — parse `field def temp : Point3 -> Scalar { source = analytical { |p| p } }` and verify Declaration::Field is produced with correct name, domain type, codomain type, and FieldSource::Analytical variant

Add field_definition to tree-sitter grammar (grammar.js) as a new top-level _declaration choice with field_source rule supporting analytical/sampled/composed/imported variants. Run tree-sitter generate. Add FieldDef and FieldSource structs to reify-syntax/src/lib.rs, Declaration::Field variant. Add lower_field to ts_parser.rs dispatched from lower_source_file. Tests pass.

Write failing parse test: parse_sampled_field — parse `field def pressure : Point3 -> Scalar { source = sampled { resolution = 100 interpolation = linear } }` and verify FieldSource::Sampled with correct config key-value pairs
Ensure sampled field source parsing works in ts_parser.rs lower_field_source method — handle field_sample_config children. Make parse_sampled_field test pass.
Write failing parse test: parse_composed_field — parse `field def combined : Point3 -> Vector3 { source = composed { |f, g| |p| f(g(p)) } }` and verify FieldSource::Composed with lambda body
Ensure composed and imported field source parsing works. Make parse_composed_field test pass.
Write failing parse test: parse_pub_field — parse `pub field def temp : Point3 -> Scalar { source = analytical { |p| p } }` and verify is_pub=true
Ensure pub keyword handling in field parsing. Make parse_pub_field test pass.
Write failing test: type_field_variant — verify Type::Field { domain, codomain } can be constructed, displayed, compared for equality, and is not numeric
Add Type::Field { domain: Box<Type>, codomain: Box<Type> } variant to reify-types/src/ty.rs. Update Display impl, is_numeric(), as_name(). Make test pass.
Write failing test: value_field_variant — verify Value::Field can be constructed with analytical lambda source and has correct content_hash behavior
Add Value::Field variant to reify-types/src/value.rs with domain_type, codomain_type, source kind, and stored lambda/data. Update content_hash, Display, PartialEq/Ord impls. Make test pass.

Write failing compile test: compile_field_analytical — compile a field def with analytical source, verify CompiledModule has the field with correct domain/codomain types and compiled lambda expression

Add CompiledField struct to reify-compiler. Add fields vec to CompiledModule. Add field compilation in compile() function — resolve domain/codomain types, compile source expression (analytical -> lambda compilation). Dispatch Declaration::Field in compile(). Make test pass.
Write failing compile test: compile_field_sampled — compile a field with sampled source, verify configs are compiled correctly
Handle sampled field source compilation — compile config key-value pairs. Make test pass.
Write failing compile test: compile_field_compose_type_check_valid — compose Field<A,B> with Field<B,C> should type-check and produce Field<A,C>
Implement field composition type checking in compiler — when a composed field references two other fields, verify codomain of first matches domain of second. Make test pass.
Write failing compile test: compile_field_compose_type_mismatch — compose Field<A,B> with Field<C,D> where B!=C should produce a type error diagnostic
Emit diagnostic error when field composition has mismatched types. Make test pass.
Write failing eval test: eval_analytical_field_at_point — define an analytical field, evaluate it (should produce a Value::Field), then call sample(field, point) and verify the result

Add field evaluation in Engine::eval() — process compiled fields, evaluate analytical source lambda, store as Value::Field. Add 'sample' to stdlib that applies a field's lambda to a point argument. Make test pass.
Write failing eval test: eval_sample_field_point — use sample(field, point) stdlib function and verify it returns the codomain value correctly
Complete the sample() stdlib implementation to properly invoke the field's stored lambda with the given point argument. Make test pass.
Write failing stdlib test: gradient_scalar_field — gradient(field) on a scalar field should return a vector field (or Undef as initial stub)

Add gradient, divergence, curl field operations to stdlib. These are stub implementations that return Undef for now (numeric differentiation requires further infrastructure), but the dispatch and type signature are correct. Make test pass.

design_decisions.decision: Field declarations are top-level, not member-level
design_decisions.rationale: The task spec shows `field def name : Domain -> Codomain { ... }` which matches the pattern of structure/function/trait declarations. Fields define standalone typed mappings, not structure members. They can be referenced by structures but exist independently.

design_decisions.decision: Value::Field stores the lambda closure + type metadata rather than pre-computed data
design_decisions.rationale: Analytical fields are inherently lazy (evaluated at query points). Sampled fields store grid data. Both benefit from storing the source representation and evaluating on-demand via sample().

design_decisions.decision: Field composition type checking done at compile time, not eval time
design_decisions.rationale: Type mismatches in composition (codomain of first != domain of second) are static errors. Catching them at compile time follows the existing pattern where the compiler produces diagnostics for type errors.

design_decisions.decision: Gradient/divergence/curl are initial stubs returning Undef
design_decisions.rationale: Full numeric differentiation requires either symbolic differentiation or finite-difference infrastructure. The stdlib entry points and type signatures can be established now; actual computation can be implemented in a follow-up task.

design_decisions.decision: CompiledField stored in a new vec on CompiledModule, parallel to functions/traits
design_decisions.rationale: Fields are not topology templates (they don't have value cells, constraints, realizations). They are more like functions — standalone compiled entities. A separate vec keeps the data model clean.

design_decisions.decision: Field grammar uses 'field def' keyword pair (not just 'field') to avoid ambiguity
design_decisions.rationale: The task spec shows `field def name`, matching the `structure def` pattern. The 'def' keyword disambiguates from potential future uses of 'field' keyword in other contexts.

reuse.how: Follow the same pattern as structure_definition, function_definition for field_definition: optional('pub'), keyword seq, identifier, type expressions, braced body
Add lower_field method following the pattern of lower_structure/lower_function: extract named fields, handle optional children, create FieldDef struct
Reuse existing type resolution for field domain/codomain types; types like Length, Scalar, Real already supported
Analytical and composed field sources contain lambda expressions — reuse the existing lambda compilation path that produces CompiledExprKind::Lambda
Field evaluation for analytical sources produces Value::Lambda; sample() invokes it using the same lambda application mechanism
Add gradient/divergence/curl/sample as new match arms in eval_builtin, following the unary/binary helper pattern
Copy the parse helper pattern for field_tests.rs; copy the compile helper pattern for field_compile_tests.rs
FieldDef and CompiledField use the same span/hash/visibility patterns as other declaration types
